# Vibe Flags

> Offline-first feature flag toolkit that lives in your HTML. No servers. No config files. No build step. Made for agents and LLMs.

Vibe Flags is a set of web components for managing feature flags entirely in the browser. Add one CDN script tag, write HTML, and toggle features with a floating toolbar. State persists in localStorage.

## Quick Start

```html
<script type="module" src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@vibe-flags/core/dist/vibe-flags.cdn.mjs"></script>

<vibe-flag-boolean name="showBanner" description="Welcome banner" value="true">
  <div>Welcome to the beta!</div>
</vibe-flag-boolean>

<vibe-toolbar></vibe-toolbar>
```

## Core Concepts

- **Boolean flags** (`<vibe-flag-boolean>`): On/off toggles, start as `false`
- **Select flags** (`<vibe-flag-select>` + `<vibe-flag-option>`): Multi-option picker, starts as first option
- **Toolbar** (`<vibe-toolbar>`): Floating sidebar that auto-discovers all registered flags
- **Store API** (`flagStore`): Imperative JS API for programmatic flag management

## Key Features

- No backend, no login, no config files
- ~11KB gzipped
- Framework-agnostic (React, Vue, Svelte, plain HTML)
- Persistent state via localStorage
- Reactive event system (`vibe-flags-changed` global CustomEvent + per-flag `vibe-flags:{key}:changed` events + `window.postMessage` bridge)
- postMessage integration for iframes, browser extensions, and micro-frontends
- Full TypeScript support

## Installation

CDN (recommended):
```html
<script type="module" src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@vibe-flags/core/dist/vibe-flags.cdn.mjs"></script>
```

npm:
```bash
npm install @vibe-flags/core
```

- [Getting Started](/docs/getting-started)

- [Components API](/api/components)
- [Store API](/api/store)
- [Types](/api/types)
- [Basic Example](/examples/basic)
- [Boolean Flags Example](/examples/boolean-flags)
- [Select Flags Example](/examples/select-flags)
- [Full documentation for LLMs](/llms-full.txt)

## Per-Flag Event Bus

Subscribe to a specific flag without filtering a global event:
```js
// Using the convenience method (returns unsubscribe fn):
const unsub = flagStore.on('dark-mode', ({ key, value, previousValue }) => { /* ... */ });
unsub(); // remove listener

// Or using explicit off():
function handler({ value }) { document.body.classList.toggle('dark', value); }
flagStore.on('dark-mode', handler);
flagStore.off('dark-mode', handler);

// Or listen directly on window:
window.addEventListener('vibe-flags:dark-mode:changed', (e) => {
  const { key, value, previousValue } = e.detail;
});
```

Event detail: `{ key, value, previousValue }`. Fires on `set()`, on `reset()` (only for flags whose value changed), and on `register()` if a persisted value differs from the flag's default.

## postMessage Bridge

On every flag change, emits `window.postMessage` with:
```json
{ "type": "vibe-flags:changed", "key": "dark-mode", "value": true, "previousValue": false, "allFlags": { "dark-mode": true } }
```
On reset, emits `{ "type": "vibe-flags:reset", "allFlags": { ... } }`.

Configure target origin: `flagStore.configure({ postMessageOrigin: 'https://example.com' })`. Default is `"*"`.

Listen in iframes, browser extensions, or micro-frontends:
```js
window.addEventListener('message', (e) => {
  if (e.data?.type === 'vibe-flags:changed') { /* react to flag change */ }
});
```

- GitHub: https://github.com/Meldiron/vibe-flags
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@vibe-flags/core
- License: MIT
